questions: What current authoritative source defines accepted Australian butterfly scope?
What current official APIs expose ALA, GBIF, and iNaturalist taxon identifiers and match diagnostics?
facts_used: AFD exposes an Australian Papilionoidea checklist hierarchy.
ALA namematching returns a taxon concept identifier and match diagnostics.
GBIF species matching returns usage, classification, match type, confidence, status, and synonym state.
iNaturalist asks API clients to use the supported API, identify themselves, and keep requests to about one per second.
inferences: AFD checklist membership is used as accepted Australian scope; cross-provider equivalence still requires explicit reconciliation.
An occurrence-derived provider facet would be evidence availability, not an accepted taxonomic checklist.
fallback_tool: official-source web search and direct HTTPS retrieval

questions: Does the current AFD Papilionoidea checklist cover the configured Australian butterfly ranks?
What licence and citation apply to the acquired checklist material?

sources.title: How to cite ABRS online resources

facts_used: The frozen AFD hierarchy contains all seven configured ranks and six Australian butterfly families.
DCCEEW website material is CC BY 4.0 unless otherwise specified, subject to listed exclusions.
ABRS provides an explicit AFD citation format including viewing date.
inferences: Checklist nodes are assigned accepted status because they are in the AFD checklist hierarchy, while synonyms remain a separate later name-assertion task.
The pack may redistribute checklist taxonomy with attribution because it excludes logos, images, Coat of Arms material, and identified third-party media.
fallback_tool: official-source web search and direct HTTPS retrieval

questions: How can the current ALA namematching service match many classifications reproducibly?
What diagnostics and build identity does the current GBIF species matcher expose?
What provider-supported bulk source supplies the current iNaturalist taxonomy and what rights statement accompanies it?
facts_used: ALA exposes a POST bulk classification matcher whose response order corresponds to the request list.
GBIF v2 exposes exact/higher-rank diagnostics, accepted usage and classification plus matcher metadata.
The current GBIF Backbone matcher index does not include all intermediate ranks, so higher-rank results must remain conflicts.
iNaturalist publishes a monthly taxonomy Darwin Core Archive and asks bulk users to prefer datasets over API scraping.
The July archive EML states that taxonomic information should not represent creative work and may not be subject to intellectual-property restrictions in many jurisdictions.
inferences: Exact same-rank classification-compatible results are sufficient for a provider crosswalk identifier but do not establish concept equivalence for all biological purposes.
Provider misses and non-exact matches must be retained for explicit conflict output rather than resolved from model memory.
fallback_tool: official-source web search, direct HTTPS acquisition, and provider archive metadata

questions: Does conflict derivation require any new mutable external fact beyond the frozen Task 2.1.2 provider snapshots?
facts_used: All conflict inputs are checked-in provider responses, diagnostics and source fingerprints from Task 2.1.2.
inferences: No new external lookup is needed; re-querying providers would make the conflict ledger target a different source state.
fallback_tool: not needed; deterministic local derivation

questions: What current official ALA endpoint exposes taxon-scoped synonyms and vernacular assertions?

sources.title: AIATSIS Code of Ethics for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Research
sources.version_or_date: Code published October 2020; current page retrieved 2026-07-17

sources.title: CARE Principles for Indigenous Data Governance

facts_used: ALA documents a species-by-GUID endpoint and identifies the species index as the source for taxon profile data.
The AIATSIS Code centers Indigenous self-determination, Indigenous leadership, impact and value, and sustainability and accountability.
CARE stands for Collective Benefit, Authority to Control, Responsibility, and Ethics.
inferences: A First Nations language name must remain unusable for public discovery until a named cultural authority or authorized publication grants the specific use.
Storage, display, query, and redistribution permissions must be independently revocable rather than inferred from public availability.
fallback_tool: official-source web search and direct HTTPS retrieval because Valyu is unavailable

facts_used: AustLang assigns a specific code to each language in its database and is intended to avoid the undifferentiated aus code.

AIATSIS says its map shows only general larger groupings, has non-fixed and sometimes contested boundaries, and is not suitable for native title or other land claims.
inferences: AustLang is a preferred stable identifier only when the relevant authority agrees it matches the asserted variety.
The AIATSIS map cannot establish Country/community or identify the authority for a particular name.
Public display, query, redistribution, export, and derived/model use require separate affirmative decisions and must be revocable.

questions: What is the current official ALA occurrence bulk-download and search contract?
How does ALA require occurrence downloads, citations, dataset licences, quality assertions, and sensitive-coordinate generalisation to be handled?

sources.title: ALA guidance: How to download occurrence records
ALA Spatial layer 11185: IBRA version 7 regions
ALA Spatial layer 11170: Local Government Areas 2023

facts_used: The occurrence OpenAPI exposes public search and asynchronous offline-download endpoints, selected fields, extra contextual fields, quality-filter controls, and optional DOI/email-notification parameters.
ALA download guidance says legacy/custom downloads may include quality and inferred spatial information and provides a provider citation file.
ALA citation guidance says search API results do not receive a DOI and recommends the offline download path with mintDOI=true and a valid email when a DOI is wanted.
The ALA index exposes cl11185 and cl11170 as stored contextual fields; the layer metadata identifies DCCEEW and ABS sources and CC BY 4.0 licences.
ALA public responses mark generalized sensitive rows; the selected archive preserves those flags and public processed coordinates.
inferences: A DOI was not minted because creating a persistent external identifier tied to a contact identity was not necessary to satisfy the requested frozen repository artifact; the exact archive and request are instead content fingerprinted.
Disabling default quality filters is appropriate only when every assertion and spatial-validity state is retained and downstream eligibility is explicit.
A public product should conservatively exclude NC, ND, SA, ambiguous, and unspecified licence values from the committed derived baseline rather than assume compatibility.
ALA contextual labels can support the requested rollups without copying boundary geometry, but they remain provider spatial assertions and LGA labels retain the statistical-boundary caveat.
fallback_tool: official-source web search and direct HTTPS retrieval because Valyu is unavailable

questions: Can a current ALA Papilionoidea Australia snapshot be frozen through the public asynchronous API with complete citation, licence, quality, and sensitive-data evidence while avoiding thousands of search calls?
What exact licence subset is compatible with a publicly redistributed and normalized ButterflyLens baseline?
facts_used: The accepted ALA Papilionoidea root plus processed country Australia yielded 603,889 rows before public licence selection.
The conservative CC0/PDM/CC-BY allowlist yielded 236,897 rows from 53 data resources and eight processed licence values.
The archive includes the selected occurrence CSV, citation.csv, README.html, and headings.csv.
The selected snapshot contains 236,303 spatially valid rows, 594 spatially suspect rows, 6,277 rows without processed coordinates, 265 generalised rows, and 115 already-generalised rows.
inferences: The provider archive plus request, source-contract, and archive fingerprints is sufficient to freeze the exact selected snapshot without storing the required contact email.
The discarded broader jobs are acquisition intermediates and must not be committed or reported as the public baseline.
Provider labels and contextual fields remain source assertions; their preservation does not establish human verification, completeness, biological absence, or legal LGA boundaries.
fallback_tool: official provider APIs and deterministic source inspection because Valyu is unavailable

sources.title: PyArrow 25.0.0 PyPI release metadata
sources.version_or_date: 25.0.0 uploaded 2026-07-10; Python >=3.10; Apache-2.0; CPython 3.14 Linux wheel SHA-256 447df764beb07c544f0178a5f6b70ef44b9ecf382b3cdfad4c2d7867353c3887

facts_used: PyArrow 25.0.0 publishes CPython 3.14 wheels, declares Python >=3.10, and declares Apache-2.0.
The official writer accepts compression, compression level, row-group sizing, Parquet version, data-page version, dictionaries, statistics, and compliant nested-type controls used by the adapter.

The frozen ALA source includes provider/resource identity, processed and raw basis, coordinates and uncertainty, event date, per-record licence/rights, sensitive state, spatial validity, quality assertions, and contextual geography.
inferences: Pinning PyArrow and the writer controls is required for byte-identical Parquet replay; the source archive and normalized logical fingerprints remain separately authoritative.
Exact taxonConceptID crosswalk matching is conservative; unmatched provider assertions should remain null-keyed rather than be resolved by names or parent IDs.
A generalized public coordinate may be retained but only routed to the configured coarse geography; no normalization step may reconstruct or advertise finer precision.
fallback_tool: official PyPI and Apache Arrow documentation because Valyu is unavailable

Official PyPI metadata declares MIT for jsonschema, attrs, jsonschema-specifications, referencing, and rpds-py, and PSF-2.0 for typing-extensions.
inferences: The project must pin jsonschema because existing contract tests import it and an isolated frozen environment must not depend on a globally installed package.
All locked transitive packages, including the cross-platform conditional dependency, belong in the dependency licence receipt.

Resource dr30019 citation metadata contains a CC BY-NC condition while its 360 selected processed rows carry CC-BY 4.0 (Int).
Resource dr635 citation rights describe data as CC-BY-NC while its 1,125 selected processed rows carry an allowlisted CC-BY value.
Four resource citations contain information-withheld text and one contains data-generalisation text.
inferences: Processed per-row licence filtering cannot replace resource-level citation review when exact provider metadata conflicts.

Information-withheld and generalisation text must remain verbatim and cannot authorize reconstruction of provider-withheld detail.

sources.title: iNaturalist API documentation
sources.version_or_date: v2.2.0 documentation; pinned BioMiner uses v1 adapter

facts_used: GBIF occurrence search pages are limited to 300 records and queries have a 100,000-record search ceiling; larger reproducible acquisition should use downloads.
GBIF DWCA downloads separate occurrence and multimedia records and multimedia rights can be more restrictive than occurrence rights.
iNaturalist photo hosts and response fields reflect licence state; open-data-host photos are open licensed while static-host photos are not necessarily open.
The GBIF image cache is a display service with a single-connection scripted-use recommendation and does not replace publisher media rights.
inferences: A provisional reference bank must retain occurrence and media rights independently and should not download bytes until use-specific rights gates pass.
Provider-labelled metadata can seed review and diagnostics but cannot establish human-verified taxonomic identity.
The pinned BioMiner artifact boundary is preferable to copying or reimplementing provider normalization.

sources.title: Backblaze B2 S3 Put Lifecycle Configuration

facts_used: B2 S3-compatible object ACLs follow the bucket ACL and object-level ACLs are not supported.
Presigned URLs are supported.
Lifecycle Expiration hides the current object version while NoncurrentVersionExpiration deletes old versions.
Object Lock prevents modification or deletion until retention expires.
Application keys can be bucket- and prefix-restricted by capability.
Put Object returns a B2 version ID and supports signed payload transport metadata.
inferences: Public thumbnails require a separate public bucket.
Manifest publication must not assume undocumented create-if-absent semantics.
ETag cannot be the project SHA-256 authority.
Removable media must not be placed under compliance retention that could prevent the 24-hour workflow.
Lifecycle rules assist cleanup but do not prove removal completion.

questions: How should a Supabase/Postgres ledger retain all logical associations behind one deduplicated physical Flickr request?

The API-request-leading unique pair index plus a query-association index covers both foreign-key access paths.
The table should be append-only with service-role select/insert only and no browser policies.
Page checkpoints must bind the root physical request so child partition pages can be reconciled to original request links.

inferences: Only explicit transient HTTP statuses should auto-retry; malformed successful responses must remain quarantined.

Uncertain attempts cannot auto-retry until accounting reconciliation removes double-send risk.
The database must uniquely key run, request fingerprint, and retry number while requiring retry parent lineage.

questions: What are the current Apple requirements for a restartable per-user launchd agent, graceful termination, service installation, and secret storage?

sources.title: Apple: Creating Launch Daemons and Agents
Apple Xcode man page mirror: launchd.plist(5)
Apple Xcode man page mirror: launchctl(1)
Apple: Updating helper executables from earlier versions of macOS
Apple: Adding a password to the keychain

facts_used: Per-user agents belong in the user's Library/LaunchAgents directory.
launchd-managed processes must remain in the foreground and should handle SIGTERM by quickly unwinding work.
Program must be absolute and ProgramArguments represents the tokenized argument vector.
KeepAlive may be conditional; SuccessfulExit false restarts inverse/nonzero exits and implies an initial run.
ExitTimeOut bounds SIGTERM-to-SIGKILL waiting and zero should not be used.
bootstrap and bootout are the modern launchctl commands; legacy load/unload are not recommended.
LaunchAgent plist files must not be group/world writable.
Apple recommends signed app-bundle Service Management structure for distributable helpers.
Keychain Services stores small secrets in encrypted storage with access controls.
inferences: A development runner should restart crashes but allow a clean graceful exit to remain stopped.
Secrets should be retrieved on demand from Keychain into memory and never be placed in the plist, environment, or command arguments.
The checked-in development script must explicitly avoid claiming production signing/notarization.

questions: How should unequal inclusion probabilities and declared strata enter a representative dataset-quality estimate?
How should shared owner and observation groups enter uncertainty rather than being treated as independent rows?

sources.title: Statistics Canada Survey Methodology: design effects and effective sample size
Resampling Inference With Complex Survey Data

facts_used: Inverse inclusion probabilities and population stratum weights are design evidence for unequal-probability survey estimates.
Kish effective sample size diagnoses loss from unequal analysis weights but does not itself account for clustering.
Complex-survey resampling should preserve the sampling design and clustered dependence rather than resample dependent rows independently.
Supabase RLS and SQL privileges are separate controls, and security-definer helpers should use a fixed search path.
inferences: ButterflyLens should use a Hajek weighted proportion and label Kish ESS as a weight-inequality diagnostic.
Rows connected by owner or observation identity should be resampled together within their declared stratum.
A targeted failure queue cannot satisfy probability-sample assumptions and must retain null population statistics.
Quality admission should be service-only, fixed-search-path, serialized, indexed, and append-only.
